title: Add depth sweep for type-expression nesting (: type-annotation, forall)
goal: Filed by the P6 depth-coverage auditor (docs/DEPTH_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md). Type expressions compose/nest — arrow types of arrow types, nested (forall ...) quantifiers, deeply nested (: name type) annotations — but no P6 pillar sweeps type-expression nesting depth. A type checker or annotation-lowering bug that only manifests at nesting depth 2+ (e.g. quantifier capture, arrow-associativity at depth) would slip through. Add a depth d=1..N sweep that builds nested type annotations / polymorphic types and asserts they parse, type-check, and compile consistently at each depth (differential across axes; expect clean acceptance or a documented depth limit, never a silent miscompile). Natural home: the P6e metaprog/module generator (test/metaprog-depth-parametric) or the P6c nesting generator. On landing, assign a pillar to ESHKOL_TYPE_ANNOTATION_OP and ESHKOL_FORALL_OP in scripts/depth_coverage_registry.json.
acceptance: nested type annotations and forall quantifiers swept d=1..N, consistent across JIT/AOT/O0/O2 (or clean documented limit)
registry updated: type-annotation + forall carry a pillar (not a gap_task)
